senses.definition: a performer, typically female, who energetically and provocatively dances on a stage or platform in nightclubs
a performer trained in the art of ballet, characterized by grace, strength, and precision in executing choreographed movements and sequences

examples.x: The ballet dancer glided across the stage with effortless elegance.
As a ballet dancer, she spent years perfecting her technique at the barre.
The ballet dancer's performance captivated the audience with its beauty and artistry.
Many aspiring ballet dancers dream of joining prestigious ballet companies.
The ballet dancer's dedication to her craft was evident in every movement she made on stage.

senses.definition: the ensemble of dancers in a ballet company who perform together as a group, providing background and support to the principal dancers

examples.x: The corps de ballet moved in perfect unison, creating a stunning visual backdrop for the lead dancers.
The choreography required precise timing and coordination among the members of the corps de ballet.
Many dancers aspire to join the prestigious corps de ballet of renowned companies.
The corps de ballet rehearsed tirelessly to achieve seamless performances on stage.
In classical ballet productions, the corps de ballet plays an integral role in enhancing the overall aesthetic and narrative of the performance.

senses.definition: a dancer who performs a solo dance piece that is often choreographed to showcase their technical and artistic abilities, and is typically performed in front of the rest of the company and the audience
the highest rank in ballet, reserved for experienced dancers who perform leading roles in productions
a highly skilled individual who oversees the training, rehearsal, and direction of dancers in a ballet company

examples.x: The ballet master provided detailed feedback to each dancer, helping them refine their technique.
Under the guidance of the ballet master, the company's dancers reached new levels of artistry and precision.
As a former principal dancer, the ballet master brought a wealth of experience and expertise to the rehearsal studio.
The ballet master's meticulous attention to detail elevated the quality of the company's productions.
Many aspiring dancers sought mentorship from the esteemed ballet master to advance their careers in the performing arts.

senses.definition: a person who creates and designs dance movements and routines, typically for performances, shows, or productions

examples.x: The choreographer created a stunning dance routine for the performance.
He is a renowned choreographer in the world of contemporary dance.
The choreographer worked closely with the dancers to perfect every move.
Many famous choreographers have influenced modern dance styles.
She dreams of becoming a choreographer for major dance productions.

senses.definition: someone whose profession is dancing

examples.x: The dancer follows a strict diet and exercise routine to maintain her performance levels.
The dancer moved across the stage with elegance and grace.
The young dancer dreams of performing on big stages one day.

senses.definition: a dancer who performs behind the main artist or group in a musical or theatrical production
an individual who verbally guides participants through the sequence of steps or figures

examples.x: The caller instructed the dancers on the upcoming moves with clear and precise calls.
As the caller, he expertly guided the participants through the intricate patterns of the square dance.
The square dance flowed smoothly thanks to the caller's skillful direction and clear communication.
The caller's enthusiastic and engaging style made the dance enjoyable for all participants.
Participants relied on the caller's cues to transition seamlessly between different steps and formations during the dance.

senses.definition: a performer who specializes in sensual or erotic dance routines, often involving provocative movements and attire

examples.x: The exotic dancer captivated the audience with her sultry moves and magnetic stage presence.

As an exotic dancer, she honed her skills in pole dancing and other acrobatic techniques.
The club hired a renowned exotic dancer to headline their weekend performances.
Despite the stigma, some exotic dancers find empowerment and artistic expression in their profession.

senses.definition: a female performer known for elaborate costumes and dance routines in cabaret or revue shows

examples.x: The showgirl dazzled the audience with her glittering costume and high-energy dance routine.

The revue featured a lineup of talented showgirls who performed synchronized dance numbers.
As a showgirl, she mastered the art of quick costume changes and graceful choreography.
Showgirls in Broadway productions bring an element of glamour and sophistication to the stage.

senses.definition: a professional dancer hired to dance with customers in dance halls or clubs, typically for a fee

examples.x: In the 1920s, taxi dancers were a common feature in dance halls across major cities.

As a taxi dancer, she earned a living by dancing with customers at various social events.
Taxi dancers provided companionship and entertainment for those who wanted to dance but lacked partners.
The popularity of taxi dancers declined with the advent of partner-dancing trends and changes in social norms.

senses.definition: a music coach and accompanist who helps ballet or opera performers learn and rehearse their roles by playing music and providing guidance
a female dancer who performs graceful and precise dance movements on her toes

examples.x: She trained for years to become a ballerina.

She admired the ballerina’s elegant movements.
The young ballerina practiced every day after school.

senses.definition: a cohesive group of performers in ballet or theater who work together in synchronized movements or actions to support the main performers or create a backdrop for the production
a leading female dancer in a ballet company, recognized for her exceptional skill, artistry, and prominence in performances

examples.x: The prima ballerina's performance in Swan Lake received standing ovations from the audience.

senses.definition: a performer who entertains an audience by removing clothing in a sexually suggestive manner

senses.definition: a person, typically a member of a team or squad, who performs organized cheers, chants, and routines to encourage and support sports teams

senses.definition: someone who loudly promotes attractions to attract attention to shows or events, often seen at amusement parks or circuses

senses.definition: a person who wears a wig and a red nose to entertain an audience

senses.definition: someone who skillfully throws and catches objects in the air, like balls or clubs
